displayTitle: The Meter Is Your Only Sense — Prove It

beats.narration: You can't see, feel, or smell radiation. The survey meter is your only sense — so before you trust a reading, you prove the instrument.

payload.title: PROVE THE METER
payload.sub: Your only sense for radiation. Verify it first.

beats.narration: Reading an analog face is one equation: dose rate equals needle times range multiplier. Needle on 1.2, selector on times-ten, means twelve milliroentgen per hour.
Calibration proves the meter reads true: every six months, after any servicing except a battery change, within twenty percent on each scale. Expired means out of service.

payload.heading: Calibration: the rules that stop the job

beats.narration: Then the battery check, before every use. A dead battery reads zero in any field — and a falsely quiet meter looks exactly like a safe area.
A zero from an unproven meter is a claim, not a measurement. Sticker current, battery in the band — then your readings are real.

payload.sub: Sticker current, battery in band, then trust the needle.
